mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, which increases consumer purchasing power and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 131,900 thousand persons indicates mature expansion phase
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with stable labor demand
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth months produce negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 1.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises more than 0.3% month-over-month, the Fed enters a tightening regime that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.07% and CPIAUCSL at 226.72 indicate accommodative stance
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 1% signals weakening consumer demand and forward earnings deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: Negative RSAFS months produce positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month, signaling robust manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength that supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 94.8263 (index 2017=100) in mid-cycle expansion
failure_condition: Months with INDPRO growth > 0.5% produce negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline more than 10% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, indicating a demand shock that cascades through construction employment and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST monthly change < -10% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 685 thousand units, UNRATE at 8.5%
regime_hint: demand shock with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: Joint trigger produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 4.5% while payroll growth (PAYEMS) remains above 100,000 persons monthly, signaling tight labor market conditions that increase wage pressures and corporate cost inflation, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as profit margins compress.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 8.5% and PAYEMS at 131,900 thousand indicate slack labor market
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market tightening with wage inflation
failure_condition: Joint trigger produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 4-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real interest rates (approximated by FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation rate) turn negative, signaling monetary accommodation that reduces the opportunity cost of equity holding and increases asset price inflation, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.07% with CPIAUCSL at 226.72 implies deeply negative real rates
failure_condition: Negative real rate periods produce positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2% annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) contract, signaling demand-side weakness that reduces forward earnings estimates and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0% AND RSAFS monthly change < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 13,331.6 billion chained 2017 dollars, RSAFS at 400,614 million dollars
failure_condition: Joint trigger produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over 5-year rolling windows.
